trade.summary: Moderate bilateral trade. The UK exports machinery, financial services, and pharmaceuticals to Thailand. Thailand exports electronics, automobiles, and food products (including rice and seafood) to the UK. Thailand is an important hub for UK companies in mainland Southeast Asia.

diplomatic.summary: UK-Thailand relations are stable but not a strategic priority for either side. Both are members of relevant multilateral bodies. Thailand's democratic governance concerns (military coups, political instability) have occasionally been raised by UK officials. UK participates in Thai-focused ASEAN engagement through its Indo-Pacific tilt.

military.summary: Very limited military ties. Thailand's US-oriented security alignment dominates. Some historical UK-Thailand military cooperation through Commonwealth-adjacent channels. No formal bilateral military cooperation framework.

financial.summary: UK financial institutions (HSBC, Standard Chartered) are active in Thailand. UK companies invest in Thai manufacturing, tourism, and financial services. Thai investment in UK is limited. Bangkok is growing as a regional financial centre.

energy.summary: Very limited direct energy trade. Some UK company participation in Thai oil and gas historically. Growing UK interest in Thai renewable energy development.

scientific.summary: British Council active in Thailand. Thai students study in UK universities. Joint research in tropical medicine and public health. Newton Fund supported some UK-Thailand research collaborations.
